title: Writs Generated from Observation Must depend on the originating writ

decisions.question: Which link kind should the observation-lift engine use for the dependency edge?
decisions.rationale: #9 apparatus owns its own contribution contract — Spider already owns the precedence-gate kind; inventing an astrolabe-scoped parallel would teach the framework a concept it doesn't need (#8).

decisions.question: Which direction should the `spider.follows` edge run — new draft writ → brief, or brief → new draft writ?
decisions.rationale: #15 hard line on cross-component contract — the kind's registered semantic fixes the direction; reversing would silently break the gate.

decisions.question: What free-text label should accompany the `spider.follows` kind on the new link?
decisions.rationale: #13 first live writer sets precedent — unanimous existing convention makes this the de-facto canonical label for `spider.follows`.

decisions.question: How should the post + link pair be sequenced within the engine's loop?
decisions.rationale: #4 collapse to a single pass — per-record keeps each observation's post+link atomic; two-pass invites a split-state failure mode where posts succeed but links don't.

decisions.question: How should the engine behave when `clerk.link` throws (e.g. the `spider.follows` kind is not registered because Spider is not installed, or an internal Clerk error)?
decisions.rationale: #2 fail loud — a missing `spider.follows` kind is misconfiguration, not a legitimate degraded state; silent fallback would hide the drift this anima needs to see.

decisions.question: Should astrolabe's plugin-level dependency on `spider` be promoted from `recommends` to `requires` as part of this change?
decisions.rationale: #23 thinnest-slice-that-is-complete vs #31/#38 fix-drift-at-the-source; resolved toward scope containment because the declaration drift predates this brief and obs-1 routes it through the normal follow-up pipeline without being dropped.

decisions.question: Should the engine's `yields` shape change to expose the created link ids (or other link metadata) alongside `writIds`?
decisions.rationale: #18 earn new structure from a second consumer — no downstream yield consumer exists; link ids are recoverable via `clerk.links(writId)` when a caller appears.

decisions.question: Should the engine emit a link for each observation unconditionally, or only when the observation count exceeds some threshold / some future per-observation flag opts out?
decisions.rationale: #18 earn new structure from a second consumer — the brief frames the dependency as categorical; a per-observation flag is a speculative slot nothing asks for.

decisions.question: How much of the engine/apparatus documentation should move alongside this change?
decisions.rationale: #38 prefer fixing drift over letting it grow — all three surfaces carry the same now-inaccurate load-bearing claim; updating them together keeps the contract, README, and architecture doc in lockstep.
